repo.description: A collective list of free APIs

QS-01_activity_pulse.detail: Pushed 18 days ago. OpenSSF reports 2 commits and 0 issue activity in last 90 days. Maintained score 1/10.

QS-02_engagement_trajectory.detail: 419,392 stars, 45,617 forks, 4,572 watchers. Top-20 starred repo on GitHub.

QS-03_archived_status.detail: Not archived, not disabled — but effectively in slow-maintenance mode.

QS-05_ci_pass_rate.detail: 14/15 recent PR-triggered workflow runs have conclusion 'action_required' — PRs queued awaiting maintainer approval to run CI. Scheduled 'Validate links' workflow: 1/1 failure in recent runs.

QS-06_branch_protection.detail: No branch protection on master. OpenSSF Branch-Protection score 0/10.

QS-07_dependabot_alerts.detail: 403 blocked (need admin access). Dependabot config status unknown.

QS-08_code_scanning_alerts.detail: 403 blocked. OpenSSF SAST score 0/10 — no SAST tool on commits.

QS-10_community_health.detail: README + LICENSE + CONTRIBUTING + issue template + PR template. Missing: CoC, SECURITY.md.

QS-11_contributing_security.detail: CONTRIBUTING.md present (6.4KB). No SECURITY.md (OpenSSF Security-Policy 0/10). Issue template + PR template present.

QS-12_contributor_count.detail: 10 visible contributors. Top 2: matheusfelipeog (826), davemachado (683) — together 1509 commits. Next 8 all ≤150 commits each. Effective bus factor 2.

QS-13_merge_hygiene.detail: OpenSSF Code-Review 1/10 — only 4/29 recent changesets have approved human review. PR merge process is broken — PRs queue for maintainer gate, don't get reviewed.

QS-14_openssf_scorecard.detail: OpenSSF Scorecard 2.3/10. Scored 2026-03-30. Only License (10) and Binary-Artifacts (10) are high. Zeros across CII-Best-Practices, Security-Policy, Fuzzing, Branch-Protection, SAST. Code-Review and Maintained both 1/10.

QS-15_sbom_deps.detail: Only 8 dependencies (certifi, requests, idna, urllib3, charset-normalizer + 2 actions + self). Minimal attack surface — tooling is just a link validator.

QS-17_fork_star_ratio.detail: 10.9% fork-to-star ratio (45,617/419,392). Strong engagement but stars outpace forks at this scale, which is normal for curated lists.

QS-18_watcher_star_ratio.detail: 1.1% subscriber-to-star ratio (4,572/419,392). Low percentage but high absolute count (4,572 watchers is still substantial).

Security.detail: OpenSSF 2.3/10. No branch protection, no SAST, no security policy, no fuzzing. Low blast radius because it's a curated list not runtime code — but hygiene is genuinely poor.

Reliability.detail: Link validator is scheduled (good pattern) but last run failed. All PR workflows require manual approval before running — queue of 1,191 open issues suggests approval bottleneck is chronic.

Maintainability.detail: 1,191 open issues, 2 commits in last 90 days, OpenSSF Maintained 1/10. Functionally in slow-maintenance mode despite 419K star social signal.

Documentation.detail: README.md is 195KB — the README IS the product. CONTRIBUTING.md 6.4KB explains format. No developer/architecture docs, but the repo doesn't need them.

Process.detail: Has issue + PR templates, CONTRIBUTING guide, scheduled link validation, PR workflow gating. But Code-Review score 1/10 indicates the review gate is broken in practice — PRs queue up and don't get merged.

Velocity.detail: Split signal: 419K stars + 45K forks (engagement velocity is off the charts) but 2 commits in 90 days + 1191 open issues (merge velocity is near zero). The social proof and the actual throughput diverge dramatically.

absence_patterns.evidence: 419K stars + 45K forks but 2 commits in 90 days + 1191 open issues + 14/15 recent PRs stuck in 'action_required'. The social signal is massive but the actual maintenance throughput is near zero.
README opens with prominent APILayer sponsorship and product table before the community index. Curated community content became a lead-gen surface for APILayer products.

All 14 recent PR-triggered workflow runs have conclusion 'action_required' — workflows configured to require manual approval for PRs from forks, combined with low maintainer throughput, means CI never runs on contributions.

creator_lens_quick: This repo understands how to structure a massive curated list so it stays navigable (alphabetized index → 35+ categories → per-category tables with Description/Auth/HTTPS/CORS columns). It also understands that a curated list needs continuous link validation to avoid rot — they have a scheduled GitHub Actions workflow dedicated just to checking URLs. What it teaches, perhaps unintentionally, is the full lifecycle of a curated-list project: community momentum → commercial sponsorship → approval bottleneck → celebrity stagnation. 419K stars does not mean alive.
